headline: Cinnaholic Return Puts Fresh Focus On Block Square Franchise Ambitions
summary: Cinnaholic, a plant-based dessert franchise, has returned to Square’s integrated commerce platform after spending roughly a year with another provider. The move highlights Block’s traction with fast-growing, multi-location brands that prioritize reliability and an all-in-one payments and commerce stack. This client win arrives shortly after company updates on workforce reductions and AI initiatives, adding a concrete commercial data point to the story. For investors tracking Block...

headline: Meta reportedly considering layoffs that could affect 20% of the company
summary: These layoffs could help Facebook's parent company offset its aggressive spending on AI infrastructure, as well as AI-related acquisitions and hiring.

headline: Why Mastercard and Visa Are the Definition of Forever Stocks
summary: Despite a weak year for financials, Visa and Mastercard remain forever stocks due to their global duopoly, massive profit margins, and forecast industry growth.

raw.headline: Why Mastercard and Visa Are the Definition of Forever Stocks

headline: Jack Dorsey Praises Nvidia's $26 Billion Bet On Open AI Models: 'This Would Be Excellent'
summary: On Wednesday, Block Inc. CEO Jack Dorsey welcomed Nvidia Corp.'s plan to spend $26 billion developing open artificial intelligence models. Nvidia's $26 Billion Push Into Open AI Models Nvidia is planning to invest about $26 billion over the next five years to build open-weight artificial intelligence models, according to a 2025 filing, which executives confirmed to Wired. headline: Exclusive-Meta planning sweeping layoffs as AI costs mount
summary: Meta is planning sweeping layoffs that could affect 20% or more of the company, three sources familiar with the matter told Reuters, as ‌Meta seeks to offset costly artificial intelligence infrastructure bets and prepare for greater efficiency brought about by AI-assisted workers.  Top executives have recently signaled the plans to other senior ​leaders at Meta and told them to begin planning how to pare back, two of the people said.  "This is speculative reporting about theoretical approaches," Meta spokesperson Andy Stone said in response to questions about the plan.
